title: Question 1.1
statement_text: Does every non-null even-hole-free claw-free graph have a simplicial clique?
context_text: This question was raised by the authors of [4] in the context of condensed matter physics and quantum information theory, where a new method for 'solving' spin models (finding the spectrum and eigenvectors of the Hamiltonian) applies whenever the frustration graph is even-hole-free, claw-free, and has a simplicial clique. The question asks whether the even-hole-free claw-free hypothesis alone suffices to guarantee a simplicial clique, making the solvability result universal for this graph class.
paper_contribution: Answers the question affirmatively: proves that every non-null even-hole-free claw-free graph has a simplicial clique, and establishes the stronger result (Theorem 2.2) that every non-null clean graph (claw-free and containing no jewel, line wheel, short prism, or seven-antihole) has a simplicial clique.
notes: Reference [4] is cited throughout but the authors' names are not given in the extracted text. The question is fully resolved (proved) by this paper.
